pattern: With two eggs the worst-case drop count is the smallest m whose triangular number m(m+1)/2 reaches n, since the first egg can step down by m, m-1, ... and the second egg sweeps each gap linearly.
hints: If the first egg breaks at the very first drop, the second egg must check every floor below it one by one, so the first throw can only afford a limited gap.
If you commit to at most m total moves, the first egg should be dropped at floor m, then m + (m-1), then m + (m-1) + (m-2), ... covering m(m+1)/2 floors.
Return the least m with m(m+1)/2 >= n; you can solve the quadratic or just count upward.

edge_inputs.description: single floor is solved in one drop
exact triangular number n=3 still needs 2 drops
one above a triangular number n=4 forces 3 drops
n=6 is triangular T(3) so 3 drops
n=7 just over T(3) needs 4 drops

stress.description: uniform floor counts across the full range
stress.note: covers every region of the triangular boundary

stress.description: values sitting exactly on and just past triangular numbers
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    k = rng.randint(1, 44)
    tri = k * (k + 1) // 2
    return [max(1, min(1000, rng.choice([tri - 1, tri, tri + 1])))]
